_comment: REFERENCE Claude Code settings snippet — merge the `hooks` block below into your own
~/.claude/settings.json (or a project .claude/settings.json). It wires each block-*.sh guard
to the event + tool matcher it was written for (see the trigger comment at the top of each
PATHS: the examples assume the hooks live in $HOME/.autopilot/hooks and the gate scripts in
$HOME/.autopilot/gates (the default AUTOPILOT_GATES_DIR). Adjust the command paths if you put
them elsewhere; if you relocate the gate scripts, also export AUTOPILOT_GATES_DIR /
AUTOPILOT_STATE_DIR in the environment the hooks run in so the scripts find their siblings.
EXIT CODES: every block-*.sh exits 2 to BLOCK (surfaces the stderr message to the model) and 0
to allow. Run-scoped hooks are dormant unless the run-active sentinel is armed for the session.
TRACKER MATCHER: block-task-done-without-evidence.sh is matched to the Linear MCP tool
`mcp__claude_ai_Linear__save_issue`. Change that matcher to your own tracker's issue-save tool.
END-OF-RUN AUDIT: gates/autopilot-end-of-run-audit.sh is ALSO a Stop-time check, but it takes
the session transcript path (and repo cwd) as arguments. Wire it via a tiny operator wrapper
that reads the Stop hook's stdin JSON and forwards `.transcript_path` and `.cwd` to the script;
that wrapper is environment-specific and intentionally not shipped here.
